commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=4718b4e63267cb12c11f696a584ab53329ee0cf9)
commands.review_summary:
The PR restores bounded Codex app-server continuity projection for fresh/transient starts and stale-binding resumes, with tests to avoid mirrored-history duplication.

PR surface: Source +71, Tests +63. Total +134 across 3 files.

Reproducibility: yes. source inspection gives a high-confidence reproduction shape: current main empties Codex model-input history, transient/fresh paths use `thread/start`, and `turn/start` only receives the rewritten prompt text. I did not run tests because this review is read-only.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Needs maintainer/contributor handling because the branch still has a hook-ordering blocker and external real behavior proof must come from the contributor's setup.

target.title: [codex] fix Codex continuity projection regressions
target.body:
## Summary

- restore bounded continuity projection for Codex app-server fresh or transient `thread/start` turns that have no active context-engine projection
- restore stale-binding continuity for successful `thread/resume` turns when newer OpenClaw-visible user or assistant messages were written after the native Codex binding
- keep normal native resumes on empty parallel history so mirrored OpenClaw transcript is not duplicated into Codex-owned native thread history
- add focused regression coverage for fresh starts, restricted transient starts, stale thread-bootstrap starts, stale-binding resumes, and post-binding Codex mirrored transcript echoes

## Root cause

PR #88262 correctly stopped passing the mirrored OpenClaw transcript as parallel Codex model history for normal native resumes, but it also removed the separate prompt-level continuity projection that two non-equivalent cases still need:

- fresh or transient `thread/start` turns with no active context-engine projection
- resumed native Codex threads whose OpenClaw-visible transcript advanced after the binding timestamp

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.extension-codex.config.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.test.ts -t "newer visible history|consecutive resumes|mirrored transcript echoes"`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.extension-codex.config.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/attempt-context.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.context-engine.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/thread-lifecycle.binding.test.ts -t "fresh-thread continuity|without a native thread binding|newer visible history|consecutive resumes|mirrored transcript echoes|stale thread-bootstrap|native-disabled transient|dynamic tools unavailable|context-engine"`
- `git diff --check`

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Codex app-server was dropping prior visible session continuity after #88262 in two paths: fresh or transient `thread/start` turns without an active context-engine projection, and successful `thread/resume` turns where newer OpenClaw-visible user or assistant messages were written after the native binding.

Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw source checkout on macOS with the Codex app-server test harness covering restricted `toolsAllow: []` transient starts, stale thread-bootstrap fresh starts, and resumed bindings with newer visible post-binding messages.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.extension-codex.config.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/attempt-context.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.context-engine.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/thread-lifecycle.binding.test.ts -t "fresh-thread continuity|without a native thread binding|newer visible history|consecutive resumes|mirrored transcript echoes|stale thread-bootstrap|native-disabled transient|dynamic tools unavailable|context-engine"`

Evidence after fix: Terminal verification passed with `Test Files 4 passed` and `Tests 34 passed | 97 skipped (131)`. The covered assertions now require `turn/start` input to include `OpenClaw assembled context for this turn:` plus prior visible messages for fresh starts, restricted transient starts, stale thread-bootstrap fresh starts, and resumed stale-binding turns with newer post-binding visible messages. The same shard also keeps the negative checks that a follow-up resume does not reproject the same stale-binding context twice and that Codex mirrored transcript echoes written after the binding do not trigger the stale-binding continuity path.

Observed result after fix: Fresh/transient Codex starts keep bounded continuity in the prompt, resumed stale-binding turns recover newer visible OpenClaw transcript messages once, and ordinary native resume checks still avoid duplicating mirrored history into Codex-owned native thread state.

What was not tested: No packaged app-server binary or remote Crabbox lane; this proof stays at the local harness-backed app-server path for the touched Codex runtime surface.

Closes #88352.
Closes #88354.
